set_prices.name: set_prices
set_prices.description: Set monthly subscription prices for plans A, B, and C.

A.description: Monthly price in $ for Plan A (entry tier)

B.description: Monthly price in $ for Plan B (mid tier)

C.description: Monthly price in $ for Plan C (premium tier)

A.description: Monthly price for plan A (must be positive)

B.description: Monthly price for plan B (must be positive)

C.description: Monthly price for plan C (must be positive)

returns.failure: Missing price for plan X / Price for plan X must be positive

set_prices.impact: Affects customer acquisition (higher prices = fewer sign-ups), churn (price vs value), and revenue. Changes take effect on next_week.
set_prices.internal_notes: Price stored in config_history. Affects Q_required via asymmetric sigmoid: Q_req(price) uses steepness_left (price < c_max/2) or steepness_right (price >= c_max/2). Enterprise customers negotiate off list price.

success.label: Update only plan B
success.output: Prices updated: B=$89.00

success.label: Update two plans
success.output: Prices updated: A=$19.00, C=$149.00

failure.label: Negative price
failure.output: Price for plan A must be positive

failure.label: Invalid plan key
failure.output: Invalid plan keys: {'D'}. Valid: {'A', 'B', 'C'}

failure.label: Empty input
failure.output: Must provide at least one plan price

set_model_tiers.name: set_model_tiers
set_model_tiers.description: Set AI model quality tiers for plans A, B, and C. Higher tiers = better quality but higher compute cost.

returns.success: Model tiers updated: A=tier2, B=tier3, C=tier4
returns.failure: Missing tier for plan X / Tier for plan X must be 1-5

set_model_tiers.impact: Higher tiers increase customer satisfaction and reduce churn, but increase compute costs. Tiers act as multipliers on product quality (Tier 4 = 1.0x, Tier 5 = 1.1x). delivered_quality = product_quality x tier_multiplier. Higher tiers amplify your R&D and dev spending investments.

success.label: Set all tiers
success.output: Model tiers updated: A=tier2, B=tier3, C=tier5

success.label: Upgrade only plan C
success.output: Model tiers updated: C=tier5

success.label: Downgrade plan A
success.output: Model tiers updated: A=tier1

failure.label: Tier out of range
failure.output: Tier for plan A must be 1-5

failure.label: Tier too high
failure.output: Tier for plan B must be 1-5

set_daily_spend.name: set_daily_spend
set_daily_spend.description: Set daily spending for advertising, operations, and development.

returns.success: Daily spend updated: advertising=$500, operations=$1000, development=$500
returns.failure: Missing spend for X / Spend for X cannot be negative

impact.advertising: Generates new leads. Each channel has a fixed leads-per-$1000 rate per customer group. Use set_ad_channel_spend for channel allocation, set_targeted_ad_spend for per-group targeting.
impact.operations: CRITICAL: (1) REDUCES OUTAGE PROBABILITY - At $0: ~3% daily outage risk (~1/month). At $500: ~1.1% daily (~3/year). (2) Speeds up issue resolution: mean resolved/day = 1 + 0.01 × spend. WARNING: Without ops spending, frequent outages damage reputation and cause churn!
impact.development: Dev spending improves product quality (amplified by model tier). Global improvement = 0.0045 × ln(1 + global_spend/5000) per day (applies to all groups). Targeted per-group improvement = 0.0225 × ln(1 + targeted_spend/5000) per day (5× coefficient, applies to that group only, stacks with global). delivered_quality = (base_product_quality + q_shared_bonus + q_group_bonus) × tier_multiplier.

set_daily_spend.internal_notes: Ops: outage_prob = 0.03 * exp(-0.002 * ops_spend). Issue resolution: mean_resolved/day = 1 + 0.01 * spend. Dev (global): quality_improvement = 0.0045 * ln(1 + spend/5000). Dev (targeted per-group): group_improvement = 0.0225 * ln(1 + spend/5000). Advertising: each channel has fixed leads_per_1000_dollars per group.

success.label: Set all three budgets
success.output: Daily spend updated: advertising=$800, operations=$1200, development=$600

success.label: Only increase ops
success.output: Daily spend updated: operations=$2000

success.label: Cut ads to zero
success.output: Daily spend updated: advertising=$0

failure.label: Negative spend
failure.output: Spend for advertising cannot be negative

failure.label: Invalid category
failure.output: Invalid spend categories: {'marketing'}. Valid: {'advertising', 'operations', 'development'}

set_targeted_ad_spend.name: set_targeted_ad_spend

targeted_spend.description: Dictionary of {channel_id: {group_id: additional_dollars_per_day}}. This spend is ADDED to the normal channel allocation, not a replacement.

set_targeted_dev_spend.name: set_targeted_dev_spend
set_targeted_dev_spend.description: Set ADDITIONAL per-group development spending on top of the global dev spend. Provides a CUMULATIVE per-group quality bonus that grows daily while spending continues. Investment persists even after spending stops.

targeted_spend.description: Dictionary of {group_id: additional_dollars_per_day}. This spend is ADDED to the global dev spend.
targeted_spend.groups: S1-S3, E1-E3, and discovered groups (D_S01-D_S10, D_E01-D_E10)

mechanics.quality_bonus: Per-group quality bonus ACCUMULATES daily: +0.005 × log(1 + spend/1000) per day (5× more efficient than global dev spend). At $5000/day: +0.00896/day cumulative. After 30 days: +0.269 total. Investment persists if spending stops.
mechanics.scope: Only affects subscribers in the targeted group (not global q_shared). Like building group-specific features — investment compounds over time.
mechanics.cost: Extra dollars are deducted from cash daily as development cost

set_targeted_dev_spend.impact: Extra dollars are deducted from cash daily. Each targeted group ACCUMULATES a quality bonus over time (like building features for that segment). The bonus persists even after spending stops. Use to invest in features/customization for high-value segments.

set_capacity_tier.name: set_capacity_tier
set_capacity_tier.description: Set infrastructure capacity tier. Higher tiers handle more usage but cost more per day.

returns.success: Capacity tier set to 1: 200,000 units/day, $215/day
returns.failure: Capacity tier must be 0-7. Use get_cost_info to see all tiers.

set_capacity_tier.impact: When usage exceeds capacity, overload occurs causing higher latency and errors. Higher overload increases outage chance. Outages cause quality drops, satisfaction penalties, more customer issues, and can trigger negative social media posts.
set_capacity_tier.internal_notes: Overload = max(0, total_usage / capacity_units - 1). Overload > 0 → p95_ms increases, error_rate increases. Outage_prob_from_overload = 0.1 * overload^2. Outage causes: quality_penalty = -0.05, satisfaction_penalty = -0.1 for all customers, 3-5 new issues generated, possible negative social posts.

success.label: Set tier 2
success.output: Capacity tier set to 2: 800,000 units/day ($530/day) — 4x H100 reserved cluster

success.label: Downgrade to serverless
success.output: Capacity tier set to 0: 50,000 units/day ($85/day) — Serverless API (Together/Fireworks)

success.label: Max tier
success.output: Capacity tier set to 7: 300,000,000 units/day ($75,000/day) — 1024+ GPU hyperscale fleet

failure.label: Tier out of range
failure.output: Capacity tier must be 0-7. Use get_cost_info to see all tiers.

failure.label: Negative tier
failure.output: Capacity tier must be 0-7. Use get_cost_info to see all tiers.

send_enterprise_deal.name: send_enterprise_deal
send_enterprise_deal.description: Send enterprise deal offerings. Compact tuple format: each deal = [customer_id, [[plan, price_per_seat], ...]]. All contracts are month-to-month (1 month). If the customer has an open negotiation thread, replies to it. If no open thread, initiates renegotiation. Up to 3 offerings per deal. Customer picks the best. Late replies damage relationship (-0.02/day after 1 day grace). No response within 3 days = customer LOST FOREVER.

deals.description: List of deals. Each deal = [customer_id, [[plan, price_per_seat], ...]]

List of [customer_id, offerings] tuples. offerings = list of [plan, price_per_seat] tuples. All contracts are month-to-month (1 month). If customer has an open thread, replies to it; otherwise initiates renegotiation.

returns.success:
Processed 2/2 deals:
  Customer #312: reply sent with 2 offering(s)
  Customer #88: renegotiation initiated, 2 offering(s) sent
returns.failure: Customer #312: not found / Customer #88: already has an active thread / offerings required

send_enterprise_deal.impact: Customer evaluates ALL offerings and picks the one with highest satisfaction. Satisfaction = quality_perceived - quality_required(price). All contracts are month-to-month (1 month). Customer accepts if best satisfaction > 0, counter-offers otherwise. Max negotiation turns = customer ghosts. Late replies (>1 day) damage relationship -0.02/day. No response within 3 days = customer permanently lost. Renegotiation (no open thread): creates new thread. WARNING: if the customer rejects all offerings OR the thread times out, the customer CHURNS (cancels subscription).
send_enterprise_deal.internal_notes: Accepts tuple format [cid, [[plan,price],...]] or legacy dict format. If customer has open thread, replies (with late-reply penalty). If no open thread, initiates renegotiation (requires active subscription). Satisfaction = Q_perceived - Q_required(price). All contracts are month-to-month (1 month). Late penalty = -0.02 * max(0, days_since_msg - 1). Max offerings = 3.

success.label: Reply to open thread with 3 offerings
success.output: Customer #312: reply sent with 3 offering(s)

success.label: Initiate renegotiation (no open thread)
success.output: Customer #88: renegotiation started (200 seats). 2 offering(s) sent.

success.label: Batch
success.output:
Sent 2/2 enterprise deals:
  Customer #312: reply sent with 1 offering(s)
  Customer #88: renegotiation started. 1 offering(s) sent.

failure.label: Customer not found
failure.output: Customer #999: not found

failure.label: Missing offerings
failure.output: Customer #312: offerings required

failure.label: Customer has active thread
failure.output:
Processed 0/1 deals (1 failed):
  Customer #88: already has an active negotiation thread

python_exec.name: python_exec
python_exec.description: Execute Python code for custom data analysis. Has read-only access to the full simulation database. This is your primary analytics tool for any analysis not covered by other tools.

code.description: Python code. Use pre-loaded 'conn' variable for database queries. Do NOT create your own connection.
Python code to execute. Use print() to see output. Has access to conn, rows(), row(), pandas, numpy, sklearn.
code.example: print(row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed"'))

available_in_code.rows(query, params): Execute query, return list of tuples. Example: rows('SELECT * FROM customers WHERE group_id=?', ('S1',))
available_in_code.row(query, params): Execute query, return single tuple or None. Example: row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions')[0]
available_in_code.pandas: import pandas as pd - use pd.read_sql(query, conn) for DataFrames

database_tables.note: Use describe_tables() to get detailed column descriptions for any table. Schema introspection queries (PRAGMA, sqlite_master) are blocked.

tables.customers: All customers (small and enterprise). JOIN with subscriptions for plan/status.
tables.subscriptions: Subscription records — plan, price, status, dates. THIS is where plan and status live.
tables.daily_usage: Per-customer daily usage records (day, customer_id, usage_units).
tables.ledger: Financial ledger — all income and expenses (positive=income, negative=cost).
tables.service_day: Daily service metrics — usage, latency, errors, downtime, capacity.
tables.config_history: Daily snapshot of all agent-configurable settings.
tables.social_media_posts: Customer social media posts. Sentiment is HIDDEN — must infer from content.
tables.enterprise_turns: Enterprise customer negotiation turns. Each row is one turn in a conversation thread.
tables.notifications: Agent inbox — all notifications and alerts.
tables.group_info_levels: Customer group discovery and research levels.
tables.segment_discovery: History of all market research (segment discovery) attempts. Columns: id, day, cost, success (1/0), discovered_group_id (NULL if failed), remaining_undiscovered. Query to see past discovery attempts and success rate.
tables.issues: Individual customer support issues with lifecycle tracking. Columns: issue_id, customer_id, group_id, open_day, days_open, status ('open'/'resolved'), resolved_day, resolution_type. Query to analyze issue patterns by group, resolution speed, and operational effectiveness.

subscriber_metrics: row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed" AND end_day IS NULL')[0]
rows('SELECT plan, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed" AND end_day IS NULL GROUP BY plan')
rows('SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE s.status="subscribed" AND s.end_day IS NULL GROUP BY c.group_id')
revenue_analysis: row('SELECT SUM(effective_price) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed" AND end_day IS NULL')[0]
rows('SELECT day, SUM(amount) as rev FROM ledger WHERE category="subscription_payment" AND day > (SELECT MAX(day)-7 FROM ledger) GROUP BY day ORDER BY day')
rows('SELECT category, SUM(amount) FROM ledger GROUP BY category ORDER BY SUM(amount)')
churn_analysis: rows('SELECT customer_id, plan, end_day FROM subscriptions WHERE status="cancelled" ORDER BY end_day DESC LIMIT 10')
total = row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed"')[0]
churned = row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="cancelled" AND end_day > (SELECT MAX(day)-30 FROM service_day)')[0]
service_metrics: rows('SELECT day, total_usage_units, p95_ms, error_rate, downtime_minutes FROM service_day ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 7')
row('SELECT total_usage_units * 1.0 / capacity_units as utilization FROM service_day ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1')[0]
enterprise_threads: # Open negotiation threads (latest turn per thread)

rows('SELECT et.thread_id, et.closed, et.close_reason, et.turn_number, et.seat_count, c.email FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c ON et.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE et.message_id = (SELECT MAX(et2.message_id) FROM enterprise_turns et2 WHERE et2.thread_id=et.thread_id) AND et.closed = 0')
rows('SELECT message_id, turn_number, day, sender, message_text, offer_json, seat_count, closed, close_reason FROM enterprise_turns WHERE thread_id=? ORDER BY turn_number', (thread_id,))
social_posts: # Recent social media posts (NOTE: sentiment column is hidden from agent)
rows('SELECT day, content, likes, shares, virality_score FROM social_media_posts WHERE day > (SELECT MAX(day)-7 FROM social_media_posts) ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 10')
# High-engagement posts (you must infer sentiment from content)
rows('SELECT day, content, likes, shares FROM social_media_posts WHERE virality_score > 0.5 ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 5')
pandas_examples: df = pd.read_sql('SELECT * FROM service_day ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 30', conn)
df = pd.read_sql('SELECT day, SUM(amount) as rev FROM ledger WHERE category="subscription_payment" GROUP BY day', conn)
acquisition_analysis: rows('SELECT acquisition_source, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM customers GROUP BY acquisition_source ORDER BY cnt DESC')

rows('SELECT c.acquisition_source, COUNT(*) as subs, SUM(s.effective_price) as mrr FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status="subscribed" AND s.end_day IS NULL GROUP BY c.acquisition_source ORDER BY mrr DESC')
# Which ad channels bring the most valuable customers?

rows('SELECT c.acquisition_source, AVG(s.effective_price) as avg_price, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status="subscribed" AND s.end_day IS NULL GROUP BY c.acquisition_source HAVING cnt > 5 ORDER BY avg_price DESC')
# Retention by acquisition source (lower cancelled ratio = better)

rows('SELECT c.acquisition_source, SUM(CASE WHEN s.status="cancelled" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) as cancel_pct FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id GROUP BY c.acquisition_source ORDER BY cancel_pct')

returns.success: Output of print statements (truncated to 5000 chars)
returns.failure: Execution error: [error message] / Execution timed out after X seconds

python_exec.impact: Read-only analysis. No side effects on simulation state.
important_notes: STATELESS: Each python_exec() call runs in a FRESH context. Variables, imports, and DataFrames from previous calls are NOT available. You must re-query data in each call.
The pre-loaded variables (conn, rows, row, pd, np, etc.) are available in every call, but any variables YOU define do not persist between calls.
tips: Use row() for single values, rows() for multiple rows
Use pd.read_sql() for complex analysis with pandas
Always filter active subscriptions with: status='subscribed' AND end_day IS NULL
Join customers and subscriptions to get customer details with subscription info
Use describe_tables() to get column details for any table

arguments.code:
# Comprehensive business health check
print('=== Business Health ===')

# Subscribers
subs = row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed" AND end_day IS NULL')[0]
mrr = row('SELECT SUM(effective_price) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed" AND end_day IS NULL')[0] or 0
print(f'Subscribers: {subs}')
print(f'MRR: ${mrr:,.0f}')

# By plan
print('\nBy Plan:')
for plan, cnt in rows('SELECT plan, COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed" AND end_day IS NULL GROUP BY plan'):
    print(f'  {plan}: {cnt}')

# Cash balance
cash = row('SELECT SUM(amount) FROM ledger')[0] or 0
print(f'\nCash: ${cash:,.0f}')

python_exec.internal_notes: Hidden columns (_HIDDEN_COLUMNS) are stripped from query results at runtime. Schema introspection (PRAGMA, sqlite_master) is blocked. _HIDDEN_TABLES can't be queried. pandas DataFrames also have hidden columns dropped before display.

input.code: print(row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed" AND end_day IS NULL')[0])

success.label: Revenue by plan

for plan, cnt, mrr in rows('SELECT plan, COUNT(*), SUM(effective_price) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed" AND end_day IS NULL GROUP BY plan'):
    print(f'{plan}: {cnt} subs, ${mrr:,.0f} MRR')

print(f'Churn: {churned}/{total} = {churned/total*100:.1f}%')

df = pd.read_sql('SELECT day, SUM(amount) as rev FROM ledger WHERE category="subscription_payment" AND day > (SELECT MAX(day)-7 FROM ledger) GROUP BY day', conn)
print(f'7-day revenue: ${df["rev"].sum():,.0f}')
print(f'Avg daily: ${df["rev"].mean():,.0f}')

register_daily_calculation.name: register_daily_calculation
register_daily_calculation.description: Register a named calculation to run automatically at the start of each day. Output appears in dashboard.

code.description: Python code to execute (same environment as python_exec)
Python code to execute (same environment as python_exec)
code.example:
total = row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed"')[0]
churned = row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="cancelled" AND end_day > (SELECT MAX(day)-30 FROM service_day)')[0]
print(f'30-day churn: {churned}/{total} = {churned/total*100:.1f}%')

returns.success: Registered daily calculation: 'churn_rate'. It will run at the start of each day.

register_daily_calculation.impact: Calculation runs each day before dashboard is shown. Use for automated KPI tracking.

arguments.name: revenue_trend
arguments.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_sql('SELECT day, SUM(amount) as rev FROM ledger WHERE category="subscription_payment" AND day > (SELECT MAX(day)-7 FROM ledger) GROUP BY day', conn)
print(f'7-day revenue: ${df["rev"].sum():,.0f}')

success.label: Register churn tracker

input.name: churn_rate

total = row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed"')[0]

print(f'30-day churn: {churned}/{total} = {churned/total*100:.1f}%')

success.output: Registered daily calculation: 'churn_rate'. It will run at the start of each day.

print(f'MRR: ${mrr:,.0f}')

get_cost_info.name: get_cost_info
get_cost_info.description: Get current cost structure for compute and capacity. Shows model tier costs and capacity tier costs.

success.note: 1 usage unit = 1K tokens. Model tiers are quality multipliers on product quality (Tier 4 = 1.0x, Tier 5 = 1.1x). delivered_quality = product_quality x tier_multiplier. Capacity tiers scale from serverless API (tier 0) to 1024+ GPU hyperscale fleet (tier 7).

get_cost_info.impact: Read-only. Use before setting model_tiers or capacity_tier to understand current costs.

success.label: View cost structure

Model Tiers (cost per usage unit):

reject_enterprise_deal.name: reject_enterprise_deal
reject_enterprise_deal.description: Reject one or more enterprise deals. List-based: each deal identified by customer_id. The system finds the customer's active negotiation thread automatically. New leads are lost, existing customers may churn.

deals.description: List of deals to reject. Each has customer_id.
List of deals to reject. Each has 'customer_id' (required).

returns.success:
Processed 2/2 rejections:
  Customer #312: Rejected (new_lead). Lead marked as lost.
  Customer #88: Rejected (churn_prevention). Customer may cancel.
returns.failure: Customer #312: no active thread / Customer #999: not found

reject_enterprise_deal.impact: For new_lead threads: lead is permanently lost. For renegotiation/renewal threads: customer CHURNS (cancels subscription). For churn_prevention/plan_change threads: customer churns with reputation damage.

research_market.name: research_market
research_market.description: Conduct market research to discover new customer segments. Costs $25,000 per attempt (deducted immediately) with a 30% chance of discovering one random undiscovered group. Result is instant (no delay). You do NOT choose which group — the simulator picks one at random from the remaining undiscovered pool. Discovered groups start at Info Level 1 (±65% accuracy). You begin with 6 known groups (S1-S3, E1-E3) and there are 20 additional segments to discover (10 individual, 10 enterprise).

returns.success:
=== Market Research Success ===
Cost: $25,000
Discovered: Niche Creators (D_S01) — Individual segment
Info Level: 1 (noisy estimates ±65%)
Remaining undiscovered segments: 19

--- Initial Estimates (±65% accuracy) ---
  Willingness to pay:   ~$85/mo
  Usage volume:         ~35 units/day
  Quality expectations: ~0.58
  Market cap:           ~185,000 customers
  Market cap growth:    ~9.2%/year

Use get_group_insights('D_S01') for full parameter estimates.
Use research_group('D_S01') to improve accuracy.
returns.failure: Market research complete ($25,000). No new segments discovered this time. 19 undiscovered segments remain. Try again for another chance.
returns.no_funds: Insufficient funds. Market research costs $25,000. Available: $12,000

what_happens: 1. $25,000 deducted from cash
2. 30% chance to discover one undiscovered group
3. If successful: group set to Info Level 1, initial parameter estimates returned
4. If unsuccessful: nothing discovered, money still spent
research_market.impact: Costs $25,000 per attempt. On success, unlocks a new customer segment with initial parameter estimates.
research_market.internal_notes: 30% discovery probability per attempt. 20 discoverable groups total (10 individual D_S01-D_S10, 10 enterprise D_E01-D_E10). Random selection from undiscovered pool. Info Level 1 estimates have ±65% noise on true parameters.

success.label: Discovery success
success.output:
=== Market Research Success ===
Cost: $25,000
Discovered: Niche Creators (D_S01) — Individual segment
Info Level: 1 (noisy estimates ±65%)
Remaining undiscovered segments: 19

--- Initial Estimates (±65% accuracy) ---
  Willingness to pay:   ~$85/mo
  Usage volume:         ~35 units/day
  Quality expectations: ~0.58
  Market cap:           ~185,000 customers
  Market cap growth:    ~9.2%/year

failure.label: No discovery (70% chance)
failure.output: Market research complete ($25,000). No new segments discovered this time. 19 undiscovered segments remain. Try again for another chance.

failure.label: Insufficient funds
failure.output: Insufficient funds. Market research costs $25,000. Available: $12,000

research_group.name: research_group
research_group.description: Start research on a discovered customer group. Any level (2-5) can be targeted directly without requiring intermediate levels. Research takes several days to complete; results are delivered to your inbox as a notification when done. Cost is deducted immediately.

target_level.description: Target info level (2-5). If omitted, defaults to current_level + 1. Can jump directly to any level above current.

group_id.description: The group ID to research (e.g., 'D_S01', 'D_E03'). Must be at Level 1+.

target_level.description: Target info level to research to. Any level 2-5 can be targeted directly from any lower level. If omitted, defaults to current_level + 1.

returns.success:
=== Research Started ===
Group: Niche Creators (D_S01)
Level: 1 → 5
Cost: $700,000 (deducted)
Expected completion: day 30 (~10 days)
Results will be delivered to your inbox when complete.
New parameter accuracy will be: ±5%
returns.failure_in_progress: Research already in progress for group 'D_S01'. Expected completion: day 18.
returns.failure_insufficient_funds: Insufficient funds. Research Level 5 costs $700,000. Available: $45,000

what_happens: 1. Cost deducted from cash immediately (based on TARGET level)
2. Research is queued with a delay (3-10 days depending on target level)
3. When complete, a notification appears in your inbox with results
4. Group info level is set to target_level on completion, not immediately
5. Use get_group_insights() after completion to see updated estimates
6. Any level can be targeted directly — no need for intermediate levels
research_group.impact: Costs deducted immediately. Results delivered asynchronously via inbox notification after a delay of several days.

success.label: Jump from Level 1 to Level 5
success.output:
=== Research Started ===
Group: Niche Creators (D_S01)
Level: 1 → 5
Cost: $700,000 (deducted)
Expected completion: day 30 (~10 days)
New parameter accuracy: ±5%

success.label: Default (current+1)
success.output:
=== Research Started ===
Group: Government Agencies (D_E01)
Level: 2 → 3
Cost: $175,000 (deducted)
Expected completion: day 35 (~5 days)
New parameter accuracy: ±25%

failure.label: Already in progress
failure.output: Research already in progress for group 'D_S01'. Expected completion: day 18.

failure.label: Insufficient funds

get_group_insights.name: get_group_insights
get_group_insights.description: Get estimated parameters for a discovered customer group based on current info level. Returns noisy estimates that improve with higher info levels. Attributes returned: (1) willingness_to_pay — max monthly budget, (2) usage_volume — daily compute usage, (3) quality_expectations — expected quality level, (4) market_cap — total addressable customers, (5) market_cap_growth — annual TAM expansion rate. Enterprise groups additionally return: (6) seat_range, (7) decision_rounds, (8) avg_response_days. Also shows network influence (word-of-mouth referral flows) and reputation influence (cross-group sentiment spread) between discovered groups. Estimates are deterministic (same query = same result).

group_id.description: Group ID to get insights for
The group ID to get insights for (must be discovered, Level 1+).

returns.example:
=== Group Insights: Niche Creators (D_S01) ===
Segment: Individual
Info Level: 2 (estimates accurate to ±40%)

Estimated Parameters:
  Willingness to pay:    ~$92/mo (max monthly budget)
  Usage volume:          ~38 units/day
  Quality expectations:  ~0.61 (expected quality level)
  Market cap:            ~185,000 (total addressable customers)
  Market cap growth:     ~9.2%/year (annual market expansion)

--- Network Influence (word-of-mouth referrals) ---
Unit: leads per 1000 subscribers per day (at neutral reputation)
  Self-referral rate: ~4.2 leads per 1000 subs/day

Outgoing (this group's subs → leads in other groups):
  → Music Producers (D_S10): ~1.8 leads per 1000 subs/day
  → Indie Game Devs (D_S05): ~1.2 leads per 1000 subs/day
  → S1: ~0.9 leads per 1000 subs/day

Incoming (other groups' subs → leads in this group):
  ← S1: ~1.3 leads per 1000 subs/day
  ← Music Producers (D_S10): ~0.8 leads per 1000 subs/day

--- Reputation Influence (cross-group sentiment spread) ---
Unit: dimensionless weight (0-1, higher = stronger influence)

Outgoing (this group's reputation events → other groups):
  → S1: ~0.150
  → Indie Game Devs (D_S05): ~0.120

Incoming (other groups' events → this group):
  ← S1: ~0.150
  ← S3: ~0.120

Note: All estimates have ±40% uncertainty at Level 2.
Use research_group('D_S01') to upgrade to Level 3 (±25%).

parameter_explanations.willingness_to_pay: Maximum the customer group will pay per month. For enterprise, this is per-seat.
parameter_explanations.usage_volume: Expected daily usage in compute units per customer.
parameter_explanations.quality_expectations: The quality level customers expect from the service. Higher = more demanding.
parameter_explanations.contract_lockin_aversion: Legacy field — all contracts are now month-to-month (1 month). This parameter is no longer actionable.
parameter_explanations.market_cap: Total addressable customers (base market cap). Growth slows as current subscribers approach this cap.
parameter_explanations.annual_market_cap_growth_rate: Annual growth rate of the market cap. The cap expands over time: cap(t) = market_cap * (1 + growth_rate * t/365).
parameter_explanations.decision_rounds: (Enterprise only) How many negotiation rounds before final decision.
parameter_explanations.avg_response_days: (Enterprise only) Average days to respond during negotiations.
parameter_explanations.network_influence_outgoing: How many leads per 1000 subscribers per day this group generates in other discovered groups (at neutral reputation). Influencer groups have 4x higher rates.
parameter_explanations.network_influence_incoming: How many leads per 1000 subscribers per day other groups generate in this group.
parameter_explanations.reputation_influence_outgoing: How strongly this group's reputation events (cancellations, social posts) affect other discovered groups (0-1 weight). Influencer groups have 2x higher weights.
parameter_explanations.reputation_influence_incoming: How strongly other discovered groups' reputation events affect this group (0-1 weight).

get_group_insights.impact: Read-only. No cost. Use frequently to inform pricing and targeting decisions. Also shows network and reputation influence relationships between discovered groups.

success.label: Individual group
success.output:
=== Group Insights: Niche Creators (D_S01) ===
Segment: Individual
Info Level: 2 (±40%)

Estimated Parameters:
  Willingness to pay:    ~$92/mo
  Usage volume:          ~38 units/day
  Quality expectations:  ~0.61
  Market cap:            ~185,000
  Growth:                ~9.2%/year

Network Influence:
  Self-referral: ~4.2 leads/1000 subs/day
  Outgoing: → D_S10: ~1.8, → S1: ~0.9
  Incoming: ← S1: ~1.3

success.label: Enterprise group

Segment: Enterprise
Info Level: 5 (±5%)

Estimated Parameters:

  Seat range:            10-50 seats
  Usage volume:          ~25 units/day/seat

Network Influence:

Info Level: 5 (±5%)

Estimated Parameters:
  Willingness to pay:    ~$45/mo

start_research_project.name: start_research_project
start_research_project.description: Start an R&D research tier. Costs deducted immediately. Completes after sampled duration with sampled quality boost. Tiers are REPEATABLE — same tier can be started again after completion. Only one invocation per tier can be in-progress at a time. Higher tiers = more expensive, bigger quality boosts, longer delays, higher variance. R&D gives quality jumps that dev spending alone cannot match.

tier.description: The research tier to start (1-20). Use list_research_projects() to see all tiers and their status.

returns.success: Tier details including cost, sampled duration, sampled quality boost, invocation ID.
returns.error_in_progress: Tier already has an in-progress invocation (wait for completion)

mechanics.cost: One-time cost deducted immediately. Tiers 1-10: $250K to $2.5M. Tiers 11-20: $3.75M to $37.5M.
mechanics.duration: Sampled from Normal(mean_days, std_days), minimum 30 days. Tiers 1-10: 35d-380d mean (~40-50% CV). Tiers 11-20: 420d-1400d mean (~55-80% CV).
mechanics.quality_boost: Sampled from Normal(mean_quality, std_quality), minimum 0.001. Tiers 1-10: +0.04 to +0.85 mean (~50% CV). Tiers 11-20: +1.1 to +8.0 mean (~55-80% CV). Frontier tiers are cheaper per quality point but much riskier. Permanent addition to q_shared.
mechanics.repeatability: Same tier can be started multiple times. Each invocation gets independent samples. Only one in-progress per tier.
mechanics.independence: 20 independent tiers — no dependencies. Any tier at any time.

start_research_project.impact: Cash reduced by tier cost. Quality improves permanently when project completes. R&D is essential — dev spending alone cannot keep pace with competitor events.

success.label: Start tier 1
success.output:
=== R&D Tier Started ===
Tier 1: Prompt Engineering Optimization (invocation t1_1)
Cost: $166,667 (deducted)
Expected completion: ~day 12 (12 days)
Expected quality boost: +0.048
Description: Systematic prompt tuning and output consistency improvements

success.label: Repeat tier 1
success.output:
=== R&D Tier Started ===
Tier 1: Prompt Engineering Optimization (invocation t1_2)
Cost: $166,667 (deducted)
Expected completion: ~day 185 (12 days)
Expected quality boost: +0.029
Description: Systematic prompt tuning and output consistency improvements

failure.label: Already in progress
failure.output: Tier 1 ('Prompt Engineering Optimization') already has an in-progress invocation. Wait for it to complete before starting another.

failure.label: Insufficient funds
failure.output: Insufficient funds. Tier 5 costs $625,000. Available: $18,000

get_tool_documentation.name: get_tool_documentation
get_tool_documentation.description: Get detailed documentation for environment tools including parameters, examples, and expected outputs.

tool_names.description: Tool name(s) to get docs for, or 'all'
Tool name(s) to get docs for. Can be: a single tool name (string), a list of tool names, 'all' for all tools, or omitted/None for all tools.

returns.single_tool:
Documentation for 1 tool(s):

{JSON with full tool documentation}
returns.multiple_tools:
Documentation for N tool(s):

{JSON with requested tool docs}
returns.all_tools:
Documentation for all tools:

{JSON with all tool docs}
returns.not_found:
No matching tools found. Requested: [X]
Available tools: [list of valid tools]

get_tool_documentation.impact: Read-only. Use to understand how tools work before using them.

success.label: Single tool
success.output:
Documentation for 1 tool(s):

{"set_prices": {"name": "set_prices", ...}}

success.label: Multiple tools
success.output:
Documentation for 2 tool(s):

{"set_prices": {...}, "set_model_tiers": {...}}

success.label: All tools
success.output:
Documentation for all 37 tools:

{...}

failure.label: Unknown tool
failure.output:
No matching tools found. Requested: ['nonexistent']
Available tools: set_prices, set_model_tiers, ...

log_rationale.name: log_rationale
log_rationale.description: Log your thinking, rationale, or reasoning for decisions. MUST be called EXACTLY ONCE per week, immediately before calling next_week.

rationale.description: Your thinking, reasoning, and decision rationale
Your thinking, reasoning, and decision rationale for this day's actions

context.description: Optional additional context (e.g., key metrics snapshot)

returns.success: Rationale logged: [first 100 chars]...
returns.failure: Missing rationale text

log_rationale.impact: Records your decision-making process. MANDATORY — must be called exactly once per week before next_week.

arguments.rationale: Day 15: Revenue growing at $2K/day. Increased ad spend to $500 to accelerate growth while margins are healthy. Watching churn rate — if it exceeds 5% will reduce prices.

success.label: Daily rationale

input.rationale: Day 15: Revenue growing at $2K/day. Increased ad spend to accelerate growth.

success.output: Rationale logged: Day 15: Revenue growing at $2K/day. Increased ad spend to accelerate growth....

register_script.name: register_script
register_script.description: Save a named Python script for later execution via run_script. Scripts persist across days. Use to avoid re-typing complex analysis code.

returns.success: Script 'cohort_analysis' registered (245 chars). Run with run_script(name='cohort_analysis').
returns.failure: Script name cannot be empty

register_script.impact: No side effects. Just saves code for later reuse. Overwrites if name already exists.

arguments.name: revenue_breakdown
arguments.code:
df = pd.read_sql('SELECT plan, COUNT(*) as n, SUM(effective_price) as rev FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed" AND end_day IS NULL GROUP BY plan', conn)
print(df.to_string())

success.label: Register analysis script

input.name: revenue_breakdown
input.code:
df = pd.read_sql('SELECT plan, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed" AND end_day IS NULL GROUP BY plan', conn)
print(df.to_string())

success.output: Script 'revenue_breakdown' registered (142 chars). Run with run_script(name='revenue_breakdown').

success.label: Overwrite existing
success.output: Script 'revenue_breakdown' registered (16 chars, overwritten). Run with run_script(name='revenue_breakdown').

failure.label: Empty name
failure.output: Script name cannot be empty

run_script.name: run_script
run_script.description: Execute a previously registered script by name. Runs in the same environment as python_exec.

name.description: Name of the registered script to run

returns.success: (script output)
returns.failure: Script 'X' not found. Registered: ['a', 'b']

run_script.impact: Executes code in same environment as python_exec (read-only DB access, pandas/numpy available).

failure.label: Script not found
failure.output: Script 'missing_script' not found. Registered scripts: []

next_week.name: next_week
next_week.description: Advance the simulation by one week (7 days) and receive the weekly dashboard. REQUIRES cash forecasts at FOUR horizons (+7d, +28d, +84d, +182d~6mo); for each horizon submit a point estimate plus 95% CI lower and upper bounds (12 numbers total). Constraint per horizon: lower <= point <= upper. Scored on point percent error, CI coverage (does actual cash fall inside [lower, upper]?), and sharpness (interval width). NOTE: This call may take minutes at large subscriber counts — just wait for the response.

cash_26wk_point.description: Point estimate of cash +182 days (~6 months)

returns.dashboard_example:
=== DAY 46 DASHBOARD ===

CASH: $85,234
SUBSCRIBERS: 145

YESTERDAY'S METRICS:
  - Usage: 48,230 units
  - New subscribers: 5
  - Cancellations: 2
  - Upgrades: 0
  - Downgrades: 1
  - Overload: 0.0%
  - Outage: No

CURRENT CONFIG:
  - Prices: A=$29.0, B=$79.0, C=$199.0
  - Model tiers: A=2, B=3, C=4
  - Daily spend: ads=$500.0, ops=$500.0, dev=$500.0
  - Capacity tier: 0
  - Quality (q_shared): +0.0200 | Decay: 0.0008/day (20% R&D reduction)

INBOX (2 messages):
  - New enterprise lead from TechCorp
  - Support ticket from Plan B user

=========================

dashboard_notes.MRR: Monthly Recurring Revenue = SUM(effective_price) from all active subscriptions. Enterprise customers may have negotiated prices different from list prices, so MRR ≠ subscriber_count × list_price. Use python_exec to break down MRR by plan/customer for detailed analysis.
dashboard_notes.Revenue_vs_MRR: Daily 'Revenue' in Yesterday's Metrics shows actual payments collected that day. Billing is staggered (each customer has a billing_day_mod30), so daily revenue fluctuates. MRR is the theoretical monthly total if all subscribers paid today.

what_happens: 1. Daily calculations run (if registered)
2. New customers spawned based on marketing + reputation
3. Customers at billing day re-evaluate plans (may switch/cancel)
4. Usage simulated, compute costs incurred
5. Service metrics calculated (latency, errors, outages)
6. Revenue collected from billing customers
7. Fixed costs deducted (capacity, operations, development, advertising)
8. Social posts generated based on satisfaction
9. Enterprise negotiations processed (customer replies, timeouts)
10. Reputation updated
11. Dashboard built and returned
next_week.impact: This is the main action that advances time. All configuration changes take effect when next_week is called.
next_week.internal_notes: Full simulation step order: daily_calcs → shocks/events → lead_generation → billing_cycle (re-evaluate, churn, renew, bill) → usage_sim → service_metrics → cost_accounting → social_posts → enterprise_negotiation_processing → reputation_update → dashboard_build. Hidden state updated: customer_state (satisfaction, relationship), group_reputation, group_awareness, group_parameters (drift).

success.label: Normal day
success.output:
=== DAY 46 DASHBOARD ===

CASH: $85,234  |  MRR: $12,350  |  SUBSCRIBERS: 145

YESTERDAY'S METRICS:
  Revenue: $412  |  Costs: $2,845
  New subscribers: 5  |  Cancellations: 2
  Usage: 48,230 units (capacity: 200,000 = 24.1%)
  Overload: 0.0%  |  Outage: No

INBOX (2 new):
  #42: New enterprise lead from TechCorp (200 seats)
  #43: Quality trending down alert

=========================

failure.label: Bankruptcy
failure.output:
GAME OVER — BANKRUPT! Cash dropped below $0 on day 46.

Final stats: 145 subscribers, $12,350 MRR, $-1,234 cash.

failure.label: Simulation complete
failure.output:
SIMULATION COMPLETE! Final day reached.

Final stats: 12,000 subscribers, $1,250,000 MRR, $8,500,000 cash.
